mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes equity drawdowns as earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or threshold crossings show no predictive power over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1% MoM AND HOUST falls > 5% MoM
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes do not produce statistically significant negative forward returns, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows zero incremental R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising core inflation erodes real yields and forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when inflation accelerates alongside elevated policy rates, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in backtesting over five years.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter mechanically supports corporate revenue trajectories, driving positive equity returns over a one-month forward window.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns, or negative GDP quarters do not correspond to negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in non-farm payrolls exceeding 250,000 monthly additions signals economic overheating that typically triggers inflation fears and subsequent monetary tightening, weighing on equities.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 250k in a single month
failure_condition: Payroll booms consistently coincide with equity rallies, or the threshold crossing fails to predict negative returns over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling real consumer credit indicates a credit-fueled demand bubble that typically unwinds, causing equity corrections over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal does not precede equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive during credit-constrained supply expansions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, a rapid decline in the federal funds rate acts as a powerful liquidity backstop that historically generates strong equity rebounds over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS falls > 0.25pp in 30 days
failure_condition: Rate cuts during high unemployment fail to produce positive forward returns, or the joint condition yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.
